channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Halliburton wins Basra Oil contract for southern Iraq field services
item.description: Halliburton has secured a contract from Basra Oil Company (BOC) to deliver Integrated Field Management Services (IFMS) for the development of the Bin Umar and Sindbad oil and gas fields located in southern Iraq.

item.title: OEUK Requests PM Visits North Sea Energy Operators
item.description: 'Energy policy cannot be made in Westminster alone', OEUK said.

item.title: Hormuz Sees Another Ship Attack as Houthis Deepen Risks
item.description: Another tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as renewed hostilities empty the waterway, while a threat by Houthi rebels to blockade Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea heightened regional maritime risks.

item.title: Automotive Giants Test Repsol Renewable Gasoline
item.description: BMW and Toyota began a six-month pilot deployment of Repsol's Nexa 95 renewable gasoline to test the scalability of renewable fuel on existing vehicles.

item.title: Magnolia to acquire WildFire Energy in $4.1bn Eagle Ford deal
item.description: Magnolia Oil & Gas has agreed to acquire WildFire Energy, an oil and gas producer in East Texas’s Eagle Ford Shale, in a transaction valued at approximately $4.06bn, including outstanding debt.

item.title: ADNOC Approves $6.2 Billion Gas Project in Abu Dhabi
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s national oil company ADNOC on Tuesday announced a $6.2 billion final investment decision to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap project in Abu Dhabi as part of its strategy to grow its global gas portfolio. ADNOC…

item.title: Iran Says U.S. Struck Unfinished Nuclear Plant, Warns of Safety Risk
item.description: Iran has warned there is a nuclear safety risk after the United States struck a nuclear power plant that is under construction in the southwestern province of Khuzestan. U.S. forces fired multiple missiles at the…

item.title: Oil Reverses Gains on Renewed Hope of Peace in Iran
item.description: Crude oil prices reversed their climb this morning, retreating on reports that efforts are being made to negotiate another ceasefire between the United States and Iran. Brent crude was trading at $88.77 per barrel at…

item.title: Goldman Warns Oil Could Hit $120 as Middle East War Drags On
item.description: Crude oil prices could surge to $120 per barrel towards the end of the year if the war in the Middle East extends and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for longer, Goldman Sachs has warned in its latest prediction in…

item.title: Brent Nears $90 on Conflict Risks
item.description: Oil settled higher as escalating Middle East tensions fueled supply concerns despite diplomatic efforts.

item.title: Black Sea Oil Terminal Halted Again After Tanker Hit
item.description: A key Kazakh oil-export terminal on the Black Sea again halted loadings following a drone strike.

item.title: Higher-For-Longer Oil Scenario Is Here To Stay
item.description: Oil prices have been on a climb for over a week now as hostilities in the Middle East continue, and despite recent talk of a glut, the physical market is flashing signs of tightness—and higher oil for longer. Earlier…

item.title: With The U.S.-Iran Deal Collapsing, How Exposed Are Oil Markets Right Now?
item.description: Even before the 30-day mark was reached in the 60-day negotiating process that would see the 14-point memorandum of understanding (MOU) agreed between Washington and Tehran crystallise into a full and final peace deal…

item.title: Why India Could Dominate The Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market
item.description: Last year, the International Air Transport Association (IATA) revealed that sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) production represents only 0.6% of total jet fuel consumption, well below the 65% target by the global…

item.title: Oil Market Loses Its Safety Net as Iran Conflict Reignites
item.description: The global oil market is much more exposed to the latest re-escalation in the Middle East, as most of the buffers that cushioned the initial shock of the Iran war are wearing thin and unable to prevent the next oil…

item.title: Why the EU Still Can’t Agree on Its Next Round of Russia Sanctions
item.description: Many European Union diplomats thought that it would become easier to approve sanctions on Russia after the departure of former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban in May, after 16 years in power. Seen as the most…

item.title: EIA Edges Up USA Consumption Forecasts
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration edged up its U.S. total energy consumption projections for 2026 and 2027 in its latest short term energy outlook.

item.title: First Nations to Invest in LNG Canada
item.description: The Shell-led LNG Canada joint venture said it had signed an agreement allowing five First Nations neighboring the project in British Columbia to invest up to around $712 million in phase 2.

item.title: Saudi Led Coalition Vows to Protect Ships from Houthis
item.description: The Houthis, an Iran-backed Yemeni group, vowed to impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia.

item.title: The U.S. Needs 5000 Miles of New High-Voltage Lines Annually. It Only Built 392
item.description: There is an old Yiddish expression that translates literally as “stop beating the teapot” which we would freely translate as, “We’ve heard this before, so why don’t you just stop?” Well, as new numbers come out and our…
